mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows no sustained acceleration above 2.5% annually. This monetary accommodation environment reduces equity discount rates and supports positive equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with contained inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k new jobs per month, signaling weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers negative equity returns as forward earnings expectations decline over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100k for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where PAYEMS monthly change < 100k for 2+ consecutive months, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 450k annualized units, indicating simultaneous contraction in consumer and residential demand. This joint weakness predicts negative equity returns as forward GDP growth expectations deteriorate over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM change < -1% AND HOUST < 450k in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389,320M and HOUST at 479k as of Aug 2016
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances (RSAFS MoM < -1% AND HOUST < 450k), or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month moving average while unemployment (UNRATE) remains stable or declines, indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This signals positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 6-month MA AND UNRATE change < 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.59 and UNRATE at 8.8% as of Sept 2016
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction signal and 5-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) shows negative month-over-month change, signaling labor market deterioration. This dual weakness triggers negative equity returns as recession risk increases, with negative returns expected over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances where UNRATE > 5.0% and PAYEMS declines, or the joint signal shows no significant predictive power (p > 0.20 in logistic regression).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 0.50% while consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, signaling monetary tightening in response to inflation. This regime shift compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS > 0.50% and inflation > 2.5%, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) show sustained month-over-month growth above 2% while retail sales (RSAFS) accelerate above their 3-month moving average, indicating broad-based demand strength. This dual demand expansion predicts positive equity returns as forward GDP growth expectations rise, with positive returns expected over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM growth > 2% AND RSAFS > 3-month MA
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where HOUST MoM > 2% and RSAFS > 3-month MA, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization. This deterioration in productive capacity predicts negative equity returns as forward earnings expectations decline, with negative returns expected over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO shows negative MoM change for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO declines for 2+ consecutive months, or correlation between INDPRO momentum and forward 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).
